headline: WhatsNap Introduces 'Actually Read' Tick: A Third Blue Tick That Confirms The Person Understood Your Message
summary: Early testing shows the third tick is never displayed. WhatsNap calls this 'working as intended.'
fullBody: Megha-owned messaging platform WhatsNap announced Tuesday the upcoming rollout of a third message status tick — a blue double-tick with a small checkmark below it, denoting 'actually read and comprehended, not just glanced at while watching a reel.' The tick, identified in beta builds as the 'semantic receipt,' would theoretically confirm that the recipient absorbed the content, intent, and emotional subtext of the message. In internal testing across 14,000 users over six weeks, the third tick was triggered zero times. WhatsNap's spokesperson described this as 'working as intended, as comprehension is a high bar.' The two existing blue ticks have been retroactively redesigned to mean 'opened' and 'looked at the first four words before navigating away.'
